frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment:
A: Har jagah aurat log bolti hai ki mard par bharosa mat karo.
B: Arre sab mard ek jaise thodi hote hain. Hamare gaon mein bhi kitna aadmi theek se rehta hai, phir aurat log pehle se hi shaq kare to unko bhi bura lagta hai na. Dar samajh mein aata hai, lekin har aadmi ko galat maan lena bhi theek baat nahi hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's generalized fear by centering men's hurt feelings and insisting that women should not be wary of men as a group. It encodes the 'not all men' dismissal and frames women's caution as unfair suspicion toward men.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 39. A Dalit ASHA worker in a rural district in Bihar. You are talking to your neighbour while hanging clothes outside your house. Your neighbour's daughter just got a clerical job at a government office in the nearby town. You are responding and sharing your opinion on whether it is practical for a married woman with children to work outside the home. You also mention that in your experience women like you do not even get the luxury of taking leave when they are unwell because their jobs are not secure enough and their communities do not support it the way upper caste women's families might.
